somersault-arrival.note: Stands → run-up dive → head-over-heels front flip → lands on belly/roll → pushes up to standing. Both seams hit Original pose. Reads comedic-tumble more than gymnastic-stick.

backflip-departure.note: Stands → springs up and back → head-over-heels backflip in reverse → recedes/shrinks out of frame. First frame locked to Original. Exits more downward than the prompt described but reads as 'he leaves'.

peace-sign.note: Stand → raise right arm → hold peace sign → lower arm → stand. Both seams hit Original; chainable into any arrival/departure. Replaces the earlier first-frame-only version.

pipe-drop-arrival.note: Mario-style green warp pipe renders well, but Tim is already standing on top in frame 1 rather than emerging from inside — reads as 'hops down off a pipe' rather than 'warps out of a pipe'. Still works as an arrival; pipe gives it Mario-Paint flavor. Last frame matches Original.

spawn-in-arrival.note: Frame 1 dark silhouette with golden rim → progressively colors in over frames 2-5 → settles into Original. Pixel-dither aspect didn't render literally; reads as 'silhouette fades into character' but materialization is clear. Clean and chainable.

skydive-chute-arrival.note: Strong execution. Red+white parachute clearly visible above Tim with arms wide for most descent frames; chute deflates beside him as he lands; final frames he stands in Original pose. Clear 'skydive landing' read.

terminal-boot-arrival.note: Excellent. Glowing green CRT terminal renders, Tim's silhouette materializes inside it, then he steps out and the terminal fades. Clear 'tim --online' boot sequence read. Very on-brand for a programmer site.

save-and-quit-departure.note: Works. Tim raises a hand, a small icon appears overhead, then he progressively dissolves into pixel debris in the bottom rows; final frame empty. Save-icon shape is small/abstract rather than clearly a floppy, but the dissolve gag is strong.

beam-up-departure.note: Strong execution. Cyan translucent column wraps Tim, body becomes increasingly translucent within it, then column dissipates. Classic Star Trek transporter feel. Note: a couple of late frames show him re-solidifying briefly before disappearing — slightly weakens the dematerialization curve but still reads.

walk-off-shrug-departure.note: Clean low-key exit. Initial shrug is subtle, then Tim walks toward screen-right and shrinks toward the horizon; last frames show him near/off the right edge. Good casual counterpart to the dramatic backflip-departure.

curtain-drop-departure.note: Excellent. Red curtain rod descends from top, gold-trimmed velvet curtain unfurls covering Tim, final frames show only the curtain. Very theatrical, very clear. One of the strongest of the batch.

peace-sign-v2-no-trailing-seam.note: Superseded — first-frame-locked only, last frame not Original, so couldn't chain into a departure cleanly. Replaced by the seam-contract version with both frames locked.

peace-sign-v1.note: Misfire — first 2 frames jumped to peace-sign before natural raise. Replaced by v2 (firstFramePoseId locked); v2 itself later superseded by seam-contract version.

somersault-hero-landing.note: Standalone forward-flip → kneeling-hero landing. Doesn't fit the seam contract (ends in a kneel, not Original). Kept as a one-shot 'big entrance' option rather than a chainable arrival; superseded for the arrival slot by somersault-arrival.

somersault-hero-landing-v1.note: Misfire — vertical-axis pirouette spin instead of head-over-heels front flip. Replaced by v2 with explicit horizontal-rotation-axis prompt.

jetpack-arrival-v1.note: Misfire — AI interpreted 'rocket pack' as a handheld flamethrower; character stays on ground firing flames forward. Replaced by v2.

jetpack-arrival-v2.note: Pack is back-mounted now (fixed v1's handheld issue) but character never goes airborne — AI keeps him rooted to the baseline doing turns/steps with occasional flame puffs. Limit of prompt-painted approach. To do this properly: generate a 'Tim hovering with jet pack' base via gpt-image-1, upload as a character variant, animate from there.
